title: Bitcoin Price Prediction 2026-2031: Is $80K Next?

excerpt: Bitcoin is expected to reach $76,000-$82,000 by May 2026, with a potential breakout above $80,000 if institutional participation continues. Analysts predict a high of $84,000 by April 30, 2026. Current price stability near $78,000 is supported by ETF inflows and macroeconomic factors.

content.introduction: Bitcoin is expected to reach $76,000-$82,000 by May 2026, with a potential breakout above $80,000 if institutional participation continues. Analysts predict a high of $84,000 by April 30, 2026. Current price stability near $78,000 is supported by ETF inflows and macroeconomic factors.
body: For May, Bitcoin price predictions indicate a minimum estimate of $79,957.94, a possible high of $118,156.45, and an average prediction of $99,057.2 for Bitcoin
According to CoinCodex BTC stats, Bitcoin may end up hitting a new high of $84K by April 30, 2026. BTC STATS. Source: CoinCodex. By: http://watcher

The current price stability is not coincidentalOverview Bitcoin is trading near the $78,000–$79,000 range as of April 27, 2026, within striking distance of the critical $80,000 psychological barrier. Overview Bitcoin is trading near the $78,000–$79,000 range as of April 27, 2026, within striking distance of the critical $80,000 psychological barrier. The current price stability is not coincidental — it is underpinned by a powerful convergence of sustained institutional ETF buying, easing geopolitical tensions, and a broader recovery in global risk appetite. # Bitcoin Holds Near $78K: ETF Inflows Hit $996M Weekly as Macro Tailwinds Steady the Market. Bitcoin is trading near the $78,000–$79,000 range as of April 27, 2026, within striking distance of the critical $80,000 psychological barrier. BlackRock's IBIT attracted up to **$214 million in a single day**, with year-to-date inflows placing it in the **top 1% of all global ETFs**. Key support: **$73,000–$74,000** | Key resistance: **$80,000**, with $83,000–$86,000 as the next upside target on a confirmed breakout. The current price stability is not coincidental.
